pages.illustrationAlt: Text-free illustration for The Sleepy Lantern Leaf, page 1

readAloudText.Drift: A little leaf glows by the path.
readAloudText.Wonder: A little lantern leaf glows beside the path while the friends get ready for night.
readAloudText.Explore: A little leaf glowed by the path. Daisy saw it first. Dino bent down slowly. Pip smiled. Itsy whispered, “Hello, little light.”

pages.illustrationAlt: Text-free illustration for The Sleepy Lantern Leaf, page 2

readAloudText.Wonder: The lantern leaf gives one big sleepy blink, and its glow becomes small.
readAloudText.Explore: The leaf blinked once. Its light grew tiny. Dino used his quiet voice. “Maybe the leaf is sleepy too.”

pages.illustrationAlt: Text-free illustration for The Sleepy Lantern Leaf, page 3

readAloudText.Wonder: Daisy suggests helping the sleepy leaf find a cozy place to rest.
readAloudText.Explore: Daisy pointed to the soft grass. “We can help it rest,” she said. Pip nodded. Itsy clapped four soft feet.

pages.illustrationAlt: Text-free illustration for The Sleepy Lantern Leaf, page 4

readAloudText.Wonder: Dino carries the lantern leaf carefully on his broad gentle hand.
readAloudText.Explore: Dino made his hand flat like a bed. The leaf rested there, glowing warm and small.

pages.illustrationAlt: Text-free illustration for The Sleepy Lantern Leaf, page 5

readAloudText.Wonder: Pip finds a soft mossy pillow beside a round stone.
readAloudText.Explore: Pip sniffed the grass and found moss as soft as a yawn. “This could be a pillow,” he said.

pages.illustrationAlt: Text-free illustration for The Sleepy Lantern Leaf, page 6

readAloudText.Wonder: Itsy gently brushes away a tickly twig so the leaf will not be bothered.
readAloudText.Explore: A twig leaned too close. Itsy moved it with a tiny bow. “No tickles at bedtime,” he said.

pages.illustrationAlt: Text-free illustration for The Sleepy Lantern Leaf, page 7

readAloudText.Drift: Daisy asks the moon for light.
readAloudText.Wonder: Daisy asks the moon for a small silver blanket of light.
readAloudText.Explore: Daisy looked up. “Moon, may we borrow a soft blanket?” Moonlight poured down like milk.

pages.illustrationAlt: Text-free illustration for The Sleepy Lantern Leaf, page 8

readAloudText.Wonder: The leaf rests under moonlight, but one little edge still wiggles awake.
readAloudText.Explore: The leaf lay on the moss. Most of it was still. One tiny edge went wiggle, wiggle, wiggle.

pages.illustrationAlt: Text-free illustration for The Sleepy Lantern Leaf, page 9

readAloudText.Wonder: Pip hums a tiny tune, and the leaf’s wiggle slows down.
readAloudText.Explore: Pip hummed hum-hum-hum. The wiggle became slower. Wiggle. Pause. Wiggle.

pages.illustrationAlt: Text-free illustration for The Sleepy Lantern Leaf, page 10

readAloudText.Wonder: Dino breathes warm air like a gentle blanket over the leaf.
readAloudText.Explore: Dino breathed out softly. His warm breath moved over the leaf like a blanket fresh from the sun.

pages.illustrationAlt: Text-free illustration for The Sleepy Lantern Leaf, page 11

readAloudText.Wonder: Itsy counts three fireflies, and the leaf blinks slowly with them.
readAloudText.Explore: “One,” said Itsy. A firefly blinked. “Two.” Another blinked. “Three.” The leaf blinked too.

pages.illustrationAlt: Text-free illustration for The Sleepy Lantern Leaf, page 12

readAloudText.Wonder: Daisy tucks the moonbeam softly around the leaf’s stem.
readAloudText.Explore: Daisy tucked the silver light around the leaf’s little stem. “There,” she whispered. “Snug.”

pages.illustrationAlt: Text-free illustration for The Sleepy Lantern Leaf, page 13

readAloudText.Wonder: The lantern leaf gives one last golden glow to say thank you.
readAloudText.Explore: The leaf glowed once, warm and golden. It did not need words. Everyone knew it meant thank you.

pages.illustrationAlt: Text-free illustration for The Sleepy Lantern Leaf, page 14

readAloudText.Wonder: The friends sit quietly together until the lantern leaf is fully asleep.
readAloudText.Explore: No one hurried away. Daisy sat. Pip curled close. Dino watched the path. Itsy folded his legs.

pages.illustrationAlt: Text-free illustration for The Sleepy Lantern Leaf, page 15

readAloudText.Wonder: The friends tiptoe home while the tiny leaf sleeps under the moon.
readAloudText.Explore: The leaf slept under its moon blanket. The friends tiptoed home, slow and soft, with sleepy lights in their eyes.
